aiFindings.description: Agent recommended upgrading database to PostgreSQL 16 without checking application compatibility with new JSON path syntax
Agent correctly identified memory leak in the event listener cleanup but suggested a fix that introduces a race condition
Agent flagged the authentication token expiry as too long at 24 hours, recommending 1 hour with refresh tokens
Agent suggested adding rate limiting to the public API endpoints which currently have none
Agent recommended splitting the monolithic CSS bundle into route-based chunks for better performance
Agent identified that error logging sends full stack traces to the client in production mode
Agent suggested implementing circuit breaker pattern for third-party API calls that currently fail silently
Agent recommended adding structured logging with correlation IDs for distributed tracing

humanFindings.description: The PostgreSQL upgrade recommendation doesn't account for breaking changes in JSON handling
Memory leak fix introduces a potential race condition in concurrent request handling
No rate limiting on public API — this is a critical security gap
Stack traces are exposed to clients in production error responses
Token expiry of 24 hours is too long for a financial application — should be 15 minutes max
No health check endpoint exists for the load balancer to monitor service availability

adjudications.label: VALID
adjudications.reasoning: CSS bundle splitting is a legitimate performance optimization the human reviewer missed

adjudications.label: VALID
adjudications.reasoning: Circuit breaker pattern is important for production resilience — valid novel finding

adjudications.label: LOW_VALUE
adjudications.reasoning: Structured logging is nice-to-have but not a critical finding for this review scope
